title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCCLXVII. Love And Matrimony.
content:
He.     If you with me will go, my love,
                        You shall see a pretty show, my love,
                                Let dame say what she will:
                        If you will have me, my love,
                        I will have thee, my love,
                                So let the milk-pail stand still.

        She.    Since you have said so, my love,
                        Longer I will go, my love,
                                Let dame say what she will:
                        If you will have me, my love,
                        I will have thee, my love,
                                So let the milk-pail stand still.
meaning:
"Nursery Rhyme. CCCCLXVII. Love And Matrimony." is a quintessential example of Unknown's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
